Romania became country number 140 on the journey to visit every country in Europe.

It is one of those places that feels easy to travel through but difficult to summarize. Small towns with quiet squares, cafés that invite you to stay longer than planned, historic castles scattered across the countryside, and churches appearing in almost every village create a landscape that feels both familiar and distinct.

Most of the country is relatively flat, but the Carpathian Mountains change the rhythm of the journey completely. Roads like the Transfăgărășan cross high ridgelines and valleys and turn a simple drive into one of the highlights of travelling through Romania.

Bucharest adds another layer to the experience. The city mixes wide boulevards, monumental architecture from the communist era, and lively neighbourhoods that make it feel more dynamic than expected. The Parliament building alone leaves a lasting impression as one of the largest administrative buildings in the world.

Romania may look like an easy destination on the map, but it quickly becomes one of the more memorable stops along a European journey.
